creator: ian.carolan@proton.me
text:
Created attachment 248978
Intel Celeron G6900 Boot log

Hardware
Motherboard: ASRock B660M-STX (2 M.2, 2 DDR4 SO-DIMM, Audio, Video, Gigabit LAN)
Chipset: Intel Alder Point-S B660 (South Bridge), Intel Raptor Lake-S (North Bridge)
CPU OLD: Intel Celeron G6900
CPU NEW: 6C+8c 13th Gen Intel Core i5-13500T
Network Adapter: Intel Ethernet Connection (17) I219-V (Intel Alder Point-S PCH)

I recently upgraded the CPU OLD to CPU NEW.
After the CPU upgrade the system would no longer boot pausing at:
Code:

em0: <Intel(R) I219-V ADL(17)> mem 0x70a00000-0x70a1ffff at device 31.6 on pci0


for around 1min 20 secs then continues booting until it freezes on:
Code:

uhub0: 25 ports with 25 removable, self powered


Things I've tried:
1. Booting with FreeBSD 14.0 installation img usb. Results in the same boot freeze.
2. Booting with Windows 10 live usb. Successful boot all hardware recognized and functional.
3. Booting with opensuse Tumbleweed live usb. Successful boot all hardware recognized and functional.

If I disable the onboard Intel I219-V adapter in the BIOS the system will boot correctly.
Is the new CPU not supported?
